key_insight: Latent electrostatics can be distilled from foundation ML interatomic potentials into lightweight student models that retain long-range electrical response and spectroscopic capability.
engineering_relevance: This enables cheaper atomistic simulations for systems where electrostatics matter, such as liquids and interfaces, while adding access to Born effective charges and infrared spectra.
method: Train an LES-augmented student on teacher-predicted energies and forces.
Optionally fine-tune the student with additional higher-level DFT data.
Extract latent atomic charges and long-range electrostatics from foundation MLIPs.
evidence_signal: The approach was benchmarked against experimental infrared spectra for liquid water, concentrated hydrochloric acid, and the anatase TiO2(101)-water interface, using distilled students from multiple foundation models.
watch_item: Electrostatic and spectroscopic accuracy depends strongly on the teacher’s underlying DFT level and training data, so distillation cannot fully correct deficiencies in the original foundation model.
paper_title: Distilling latent electrostatics from foundation machine learning interatomic potentials
